authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service, Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Lake Lucille not listed among El Dorado Co. release waters)
Sierra Nevada Yellow-Legged Frog Habitat Restoration, Desolation Wilderness — USDA Forest Service, Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit (Project #24336)
sources.quotedText: A combination of gill netting and electrofishing to remove introduced brook trout and rainbow trout will occur within selected lakes in Desolation Wilderness to restore habitat for mountain yellow-legged frog (Rana muscosa). ... Current Status: Completed Location Summary: LTBMU Desolation Wilderness, Ralston, Tamarack, Cagwin, Magerery, Lucille, Le Conte, Jabu Lakes.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 263057, "Lake Lucille", Lake, El Dorado County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":263057,"gaz_name":"Lake Lucille","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13370816.3342, 4701747.1051) converts to lon -120.112087, lat 38.860766, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-120.1118660, 38.8608948) to within ~20m.

parameters.note: Lake Lucille is a high alpine lake in the Desolation Wilderness (El Dorado Co.), on the Pyramid Creek drainage. It lies on the west (Pacific) side of the Sierra crest and drains via Pyramid Creek to the South Fork American River — it is in the American River watershed, not the Lake Tahoe basin — so the special Lake Tahoe tributary closure (§7.50(b)(152), which reaches only flowing Tahoe tributaries upstream to the first lake) has no application here. Lake Lucille is not listed by name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1): open all year, 5/10. Fish status: the lake is documented fishless. The USDA Forest Service Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit's Sierra Nevada Yellow-Legged Frog Habitat Restoration project (Project #24336; decision 09/2008; status Completed) removed introduced trout from Lucille, Margery, and neighboring Desolation lakes by gill netting and electrofishing. No current source names any fish, and Lake Lucille is not in CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document, so species and stocking are intentionally left empty. The season/bag/license rows below state what would govern the lake body under general regulation, independent of whether fish are currently present.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water). Note: the lake is documented fishless following a Forest Service frog-habitat fish-removal project.
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.
